items.title: Canva proposes national competition to rebrand PIA under new ownership
items.summary:
Canva has proposed organising and funding a nationwide design competition to rebrand Pakistan International Airlines (PIA), inviting Pakistanis to help shape the national carrier’s new identity under its new ownership.
In a LinkedIn post on Monday, Canva General Manager for the Middle East, North Africa, Afghanistan and Pakistan, Ahmed Iqbal, said the company was seeking to connect with PIA’s lead

items.title: South korea’s nofi tenders to buy up to 138,000 metric tons corn, traders say
items.summary:
HAMBURG: Leading South Korean feedmaker Nonghyup Feed Inc has issued an international tender to purchase up to 138,000 metric tons of animal feed corn, European traders said on Monday.
The deadline for submission of price offers in the tender is Tuesday, July 21.
Chicago corn, soybeans jump 3% on weather risks and China demand hopes
Arrival of the corn in South Korea is sought in two consignments

items.title: Andy Burnham to become prime minister, pledging to 'rewire Britain'
items.summary:
LONDON: Andy Burnham becomes ​Britain’s seventh prime minister in a decade on Monday, promising to “rewire” the nation to focus more clearly on issues ‌people care about, such as a cost-of-living crisis and poorly performing services.
After Keir Starmer makes his farewell speech outside 10 Downing Street and formally tenders his resignation to King Charles, the monarch will receive Burnham to ask

items.title: Iran to pursue diplomacy based on its national interests, foreign ministry spokesperson says
items.summary:
DUBAI: Tehran pursues war and diplomacy based on its national interests, Iran’s foreign ministry spokesperson said on Monday, after being asked whether the door to talks with the US was now closed.
Iran says lack of trust, contradictory US positions, Israeli attacks on Lebanon are delaying diplomacy
“I reject the duality of either war or diplomacy. Diplomacy is a tool through which we pursue our n

items.title: DPM Dar, Canadian FM review bilateral ties, agree to hold annual progress reviews
items.summary:
Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Senator Mohammad Ishaq Dar met with Canada’s Minister of Foreign Affairs Anita Anand in Islamabad on Monday to discuss key aspects of Pakistan-Canada relations.
During the meeting, both leaders reviewed recent developments in bilateral ties and exchanged views on pressing regional and international issues. Reaffirming their commitment to strengthening a m

items.title: What the arrival of 2,000 BYD vehicles signals for Pakistan's EV market
items.summary:
More than 2,000 BYD new energy vehicles (NEVs) recently arrived at Karachi Port aboard a roll-on/roll-off (RoRo) vessel in the company’s largest shipment to Pakistan to date, indicating a growing interest in electric vehicles (EVs), even as analysts warn that infrastructure gaps could temper broader adoption.
Mega Motor Company (MMC), the official partner of BYD in Pakistan, announced the arrival,

items.title: Stocks tumble at PSX as escalating tensions take toll on investor sentiments
items.summary:
Massive selling pressure was observed at the Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX), as the United States and ​Iran expanded attacks in the Middle East, with the benchmark KSE-100 Index shedding over 1,000 points during the trading session on Monday.
At 12:34pm, the benchmark index was hovering at 174,774.96, down by 1,027.82 points or 0.58%.
Selling was witnessed in key sectors, including automobile assem

items.title: Sterling steady as Burnham set to become prime minister
items.summary:
LONDON: The pound was broadly steady on Monday with Andy Burnham set to take over as British Prime Minister from Keir Starmer, while markets keenly awaited confirmation of who will be tapped as the new chancellor.
Sterling was last little changed against the dollar at $1.3457.
Against the euro, it was 0.1% higher at 84.94 pence.
After Starmer makes his farewell speech outside his Number 10 Downing

items.title: Floods, landslides across northern India kill at least 25
items.summary:
GUWAHATI/SRINAGAR: At least 25 people died in floods and landslides caused by torrential rain across northern and northeastern India over the weekend, officials said on Monday, with more rain predicted over the next few days.
At least 15 people were killed in separate rain-related incidents in Poonch, Rajouri and Doda districts of Indian-Illegally Occupied Jammu and Kashmir (IIOJK), while several

items.title: South African rand steady as investors await central bank guidance
items.summary:
JOHANNESBURG: South Africa’s rand was steady in early trade on Monday ahead of this week’s monetary policy decision from the South African Reserve Bank, with higher oil prices and elevated risk aversion limiting its ability to benefit from a softer US dollar.  
At 0653 GMT the rand traded at 16.54 against the dollar , little changed from its previous close of 16.52.
Oil prices jumped about 3% on M

items.title: China June refined copper imports hit nine-month high as domestic supply falls
items.summary:
China’s imports of refined copper hit a nine-month high in June, thanks to strong demand and declining domestic supply.
The world’s largest copper consumer brought in 281,307 metric tons of the metal in June, up 0.5% from the prior month and the highest for a single month since last September, data from the General Administration of Customs showed on Monday.
The Yangshan copper premium, a gauge of

items.title: Regional supply disruptions force Agritech to halt urea plant
items.summary:
Agritech Limited (AGL) has announced that its urea plant has halted operations after Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited (SNGPL) suspended the supply of re-gasified liquefied natural gas (RLNG) amid the prevailing regional situation.
The listed company disclosed the development in a notice to the Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) on Monday.
“Agritech Limited informs that Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limi

items.title: Russia's Putin meets North Korea's top diplomat in Kremlin
items.summary:
MOSCOW: Russia’s President Vladimir Putin met North Korea’s Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui an the Kremlin on Sunday, thanking her for help in what Moscow calls the special military operation, the Kremlin said.
North Korean troops helped Russia repel a major Ukrainian incursion into its western Kursk region in 2024.
Under a mutual defence pact between the two countries, North Korea sent some 14,000

items.title: Captain Gill wants India to improve fitness ahead of World Cup
items.summary:
LONDON: Captain Shubman Gill said India failed to field their best combination in the one-day series against England because of frequent injuries to players, a concern the team must address before next year’s World Cup.
India were without their premier all-rounder Hardik Pandya and his replacement Nitish Kumar Reddy also missed the tour of England with a quadriceps injury.
Seamer Harshit Rana and

items.title: French wheat crop sheds 7.6% after heatwave hit yields, Argus says
items.summary:
PARIS: France’s soft wheat crop is expected to fall to 30.80 million metric tons this year, down 7.6% from 2025, after dry and hot weather hit yields in the European Union’s biggest producer of the cereal, Argus Media said on Monday.
France has suffered from extreme heatwaves since May, adding to a dry spring that followed excessive winter rain in the western half of the country.
Argus estimated t

items.title: Govt appoints Humaira Zia Mufti as PECO chairperson
items.summary:
Pakistan Engineering Company Ltd. (PECO) said on Monday that Humaira Zia Mufti was appointed as Ex-Officio Director/ Chairman of Pakistan Engineering Company (PECO) with immediate effect to represent the Government of Pakistan on the PECO Board.
The company announced in a notice to the Pakistan Stock Exchange today.
The government also issued a notification which read, “The Secretary, Ministry of

items.title: Tempers flare at end of Spain-Argentina World Cup final
items.summary:
NEW YORK: A brief brawl erupted ​at the final whistle ‌of the World Cup final when Argentina midfielder ​Leandro Paredes shoved ​Spain’s Gavi to the ground ⁠as the European ​champions’ substitutes rushed onto ​the pitch to celebrate their 1-0 win on Sunday.
Argentina ​coach Lionel Scaloni ​intervened to cool tempers down at ‌the ⁠end of a fierce encounter.

items.title: Rodri returns from 'hell' to drive Spain to World Cup glory
items.summary:
EAST RUTHERFORD: Spain’s midfield rock Rodri hopes younger generations can learn from ‌his experience in overcoming adversity after he won the World Cup on Sunday to complete his collection of the game’s major prizes less than two years after rupturing his knee ligaments.
The 30-year-old was named the tournament’s best player and ​joined greats Franz Beckenbauer, Gerd Muller and Zinedine Zidane as

items.summary:
A view of Manchhar Lake from RD-62 of its embankment, locally known as Bund Manchhar, on July 16. Photo credit: Maula Bux
    
An elderly fisherman, Maula Bux Mallah, looks out over Manchhar Lake from his small grocery shop at RD-62 on the lake’s dyke.

items.title: Boos as Trump and Infantino enter the field for World Cup trophy ceremony
items.summary:
NEW YORK: Boos descended from the stands as ​US President Donald Trump and FIFA ‌chief Gianni Infantino took to the field for the World Cup trophy ceremony at New ​York New Jersey stadium on Sunday.
Spain ​beat defending champions Argentina 1-0 to win ⁠the title.

items.title: Oil rally rolls on, casting a fresh shadow on the Indian rupee
items.summary:
MUMBAI: The Indian rupee is expected to come under pressure at Monday’s open after oil prices extended their rally, with Brent crude rising above $90 per barrel on an escalation of hostilities between the US and Iran in the Middle East.
The rupee is expected to slip past 96.50 per US dollar at open, traders said, after settling at 96.28 on Friday.
The currency has fallen 1.7% this month, weighed b

items.title: China's June US soybean imports fall 21%, Brazil shipments rise 14%
items.summary:
BEIJING: China’s soybean imports from the United States fell 20.6% year-on-year in June, reflecting the lingering impact of trade tensions that had prompted Chinese buyers to delay purchases of last year’s US harvest until after an October leaders’ summit.
Following the summit, Beijing bought around 12 million tons of US beans.
China also began purchasing new-crop US soybeans after a May 14–15 sum

items.title: Oil pain to drag Indian bonds lower
items.summary:
MUMBAI: Indian government bonds are poised to weaken in early trade on Monday as an intensifying US-Iran war drove crude over $90 a barrel, rekindling inflation and rupee worries, while the lack of an update on Bloomberg index inclusion adds to the pressure.
The benchmark 6.94% 2036 bond yield is expected to trade between 6.80% and 6.85%, a private-bank trader said.
It ended Friday’s session at 6.

items.title: Auto policy’s mixed signals
items.summary: Pakistan’s automobile industry has entered one of its most uncertain phases in over a decade. As part of the government’s tariff rationalisation commitments under the International Monetary Fund (IMF) programme, the protective gap between imported completely built-up (CBU) vehicles and locally assembled completely knocked-down (CKD) vehicles has been sharply reduced to just about 15 percentage poi

items.title: Thai baht/US dollar weaker on Monday
items.summary:
BANGKOK: The Thai baht weakened against the US dollar on Monday.
At 0158 GMT, the baht was 0.12% lower at 33.64 versus the dollar, after trading in a range of 33.645 to 33.700.
It ended the previous session at 33.6 per dollar, as per LSEG data.

items.title: Palm oil gains on surging crude prices, El Niño worries
items.summary:
JAKARTA: Malaysian palm oil futures rose on Monday, tracking a rally in crude oil pricesand strength in Dalian vegetable oils, while renewed worries about El Niño also helped pull prices higher.
The benchmark palm oil contract for October delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange was up 39 ringgit, or 0.85%, at 4,636 ringgit ($1,134.33) a metric ton by the midday break.

items.title: China's June crude imports from Middle East tumble; Russian volumes steady
items.summary:
BEIJING: China’s June crude oil imports from Russia, its biggest supplier, fell 1% year-on-year to 8.3 million tons, or 2.02 million barrels per day, according to customs data published on Monday.
China’s June crude imports slumped 41.3% to their lowest point in almost a decade.
Imports from Malaysia, the largest trans-shipment hub for sanctioned Iranian oil, fell 81% to 1.34 million tons, or 0.33

items.title: China's yuan firms against a directionless dollar
items.summary:
SHANGHAI: China’s yuan firmed slightly on Monday against a directionless dollar, caught between the global energy shock and cooling inflation at home.
Analysts expect the dollar-yuan rate to remain range-bound as strong exports support the Chinese currency but weak domestic consumption reduces its appeal.
“The yuan is likely to be highly correlated with the dollar index, and will fluctuate within

items.title: China stocks rebound on signs of state support; tech share slide continues
items.summary:
SHANGHAI: China stocks rose on Monday, following last week’s global selloff, as signs of state support lifted sentiment in the so-called traditional sectors while richly valued tech shares continued to drop.
The large-cap CSI300 Index was up 1.6% by the lunch break, while the Shanghai Composite Index gained 1.2%.
Both indices slumped more than 5% last week as a global selloff in chip shares and re

items.title: Spray and pray is not an export strategy
items.summary:

items.title: Iran's Guards say two oil tankers 'exploded' in southern Strait of Hormuz
items.summary:
The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said on Monday that two oil tankers had exploded and been immobilised after attempting to transit what it described as an unsafe southern route through the Strait of Hormuz, alleging they had been encouraged by the US military to use the passage.
Reuters could not immediately verify the incident.
The statement gave no details on the vessels’ names, flags, crew

items.title: Australian shares edge higher as energy stocks rise on oil supply worries
items.summary:
Australian shares edged higher on Monday, helped by energy stocks as attacks by the United States and Iran intensified over the weekend, pushing oil prices up and raising concerns over global supplies.
The S&P/ASX 200 index rose 0.2% to 8,809.8 by 0042 GMT.
The benchmark had closed 0.5% lower on Friday and posted its second straight weekly loss.
Energy stocks gained as much as 2.9% and were on

items.title: Brent oil tops $90 as US, Iran expand strikes in the Middle East
items.summary:
Brent oil prices rose two per cent to more than $90 per barrel on Monday, as escalating US-Iran hostilities in the Middle East restricted oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.
Brent crude futures LCOc1 climbed $2.09, or 2.37pc, to $90.19 by 0241 GMT, touching the highest since June 11, extending gains after rising 15.9pc last week, its biggest weekly gain since April.
US West Texas Intermedi

items.title: Australia, NZ dollars wobble on war jitters, kiwi inflation data in focus
items.summary:
SYDNEY: The Australian and New Zealand dollars struggled to steady on Monday as more US strikes on Iran sent oil prices sharply higher, with the kiwi bracing for a key inflation report that could help decide another near-term rate hike.
The Aussie hit a four-day low of $0.6960 before reversing the losses to be 0.1% higher at $0.6988. That followed a 0.5% gain last week with resistance at the recen

items.title: Balochistan gives Rs3 billion to railways for train project in Quetta city
items.summary:
LAHORE: The Pakistan Railways (PR) has finalised master plans for the restoration, conservation and modernisation of the Lahore and Taxila railway stations in collaboration with the Punjab government, which will provide the required funds.
On the other hand, the Balochistan government has provided Rs3bn funds to the railways for the operation of a train in Quetta city whereas the Sindh government

items.title: China to train 0.3m Pakistani youths in digital skills annually, says Ahsan Iqbal
items.summary:
LAHORE: Federal Minister for Planning, Development & Special Initiatives Ahsan Iqbal says that China will impart digital skills training to as many as 300,000 Pakistani youths every year, further strengthening the country’s technology sector and knowledge economy.
“Pakistan has emerged as the fourth-largest freelancing country in the world and the talent and capabilities of its youth are playi

items.title: 40 registered life-saving medicines remain out of patients’ reach
items.summary: KARACHI: Despite being registered by Pakistan’s drug regulator, around 40 new life-saving medicines remain unavailable to patients because the federal government has yet to approve their prices, forcing thousands of people suffering from cancer, diabetes, haemophilia, Parkinson’s disease and other serious illnesses to rely on expensive smuggled or unregistered medicines, officials and market sourc

items.title: PM Shehbaz urged to release Rs40bn required for completion of Karachi's K-IV water supply project
items.summary:
KARACHI: The Jamaat-i-Islami (JI) has written a letter to Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, expressing concern over the city’s worsening water crisis and urging the federal government to ensure the early completion of the K-IV water supply project.
